title: QueueEntry
description: A queue entry shape returned by list / lease.

entry_id.description:
Stable entry id (unique within the project queue). Mutation calls
target this id.

expire_after_secs.description:
Grace window in seconds after `run_at` before the entry is expired
and dropped on sweep. `None` means never expire. Ignored when
`run_at` is `None`.

run_at.description:
RFC 3339 earliest-dispatch time for a deferred entry. While `now`
is before this instant the entry is pending-but-not-leasable.
`None` for ordinary (dispatch-ASAP) entries.

subject_dispatch.description:
Full dispatch envelope — included so the daemon can lease an entry
and start work without a second roundtrip.

subject_id.description: Subject id from the dispatch envelope.

task_id.description: Task id, if this entry's subject is a built-in task.

workflow_id.description: Workflow id attached to an Assigned entry.

SubjectDispatch.description:
Full dispatch envelope handed off from queue / scheduler / trigger to the
workflow runner.

Carries the subject identity (`subject`), the workflow ref to run, and
optional input + variables + priority + trigger source + requested-at
timestamp. The shape is wire-compatible with the previous home in
ao-cli's `protocol` crate.

input.description: Optional initial input JSON for workflow variables.

trigger_source.description:
Tag identifying which subsystem produced this dispatch
(e.g., `"ready-queue"`, `"trigger:slack"`, `"schedule:nightly"`).

SubjectRef.description: Backend-qualified subject reference with optional display metadata.
